Belgium.status: Needs a win to control 2nd place; heavy favorite today against the group's bottom side

New Zealand.status: Bottom of the group, needs a win and help elsewhere to have any realistic path

NZL.NOTE: NZL's SOT average (6.5) is inflated by one strong performance (8 SOT vs a defensively shakier Iran); against Belgium's much tighter defense (only 1 goal conceded across 2 matches) this is unlikely to repeat fully.

context_flags: MASSIVE_MISMATCH: Belgium 82.65% favorite — the most lopsided FTR of any GD3 fixture analyzed so far. Treat this as blowout-regime for all comparison props.
BEL_DEFENSE_ELITE: Belgium has conceded only 1 goal across 2 WC2026 matches (1 to Egypt, 0 to Iran) — suppresses NZL's scoring/SOT props below their raw average.

NZL_DESPERATION_MOTIVATION: NZL is bottom of the group and needs a win badly — unlike a truly dead-rubber team, they have real incentive to commit numbers forward, which raises (not lowers) their own foul/card/offside risk relative to a pure park-the-bus read.

BEL_SOT_MARKET_ABOVE_HISTORY: the market-implied SOT rate for Belgium today (λ≈7.1) is well above their 5.0 ESPN average from tougher EGY/IRN matchups — makes sense given NZL is comfortably the weakest defense in the group.

questions.text: At halftime, will both teams have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Half-split Poisson off full-match ESPN averages (NZL λ_1H≈3.25→P(≥1)=0.96; BEL λ_1H≈2.5→P(≥1)=0.92) gives a joint ≈0.88. Both teams have shown they generate shots early — shaded down slightly for uncertainty in the even-split assumption.

questions.text: Will New Zealand commit more fouls than Belgium?
questions.reasoning: Both teams average exactly 11.0 fouls/game — a true tie on paper, and Poisson-with-ties gives 0.46 raw. But NZL will almost certainly see far less of the ball than ever today against a stronger, more dominant Belgium, pushing them toward the 'defending team fouls more' pattern seen across the tournament. Nets out close to a coin-flip.

questions.text: Will Belgium receive at least 1 card in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Belgium's card average is 2.0/game (includes a red card vs Iran). Half-split Poisson (λ_2H≈1.0) gives P(≥1)=0.63.

questions.text: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets compound market: Yes/Over = 52.37%. Trust it.

questions.text: Will New Zealand have 2 or more shots on target in the second half?
questions.reasoning: ESPN-raw half-split (λ_2H≈3.25) gives P(≥2)=0.84, but this leans on NZL's outlier 8-SOT game against a weaker Iranian defense. Belgium's defense has been far tighter (1 goal conceded all tournament) — shaded down meaningfully from the raw number.

questions.text: Will New Zealand receive more cards than Belgium?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets handicap market: NZL -0.5 cards covers (i.e. NZL strictly more cards) at 51.99%. Trust it.

questions.text: Will New Zealand score at least 1 goal?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: NZL over 0.5 goals = 56.85%. NZL has scored in both of their matches so far (2 vs Iran, 1 vs Egypt) — consistent with the market.

questions.text: Will Belgium score in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Belgium 2H over 0.5 goals = 83.48%. Trust it — consistent with Belgium's status as a heavy favorite against the weakest defense in the group.

questions.text: Will Belgium have 4 or more shots on target?
questions.reasoning: ESPN-history Poisson (λ=5.0) gives P(≥4)=0.74, but the market-implied rate for THIS specific matchup (λ≈7.1, from the Over6.5 SOT line at 56.3%) is considerably higher, reflecting NZL's weak defense. Weight toward the matchup-specific market read.

questions.text: Will Youri Tielemans have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Illiquid full-match offer (0.5128, adjusted ×0.945=0.485). Tielemans is a deep-lying playmaker, not Belgium's primary shot-taker, even in a dominant performance. Trust the market near-raw.

strongest_picks: Q9: Belgium 4+ SOT = 0.85 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — matchup-specific market well above historical average vs tougher opponents)
Q8: Belgium scores in 2H = 0.83 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — direct market)
Q1: Both teams 1+ SOT at HT = 0.82 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — both teams have shown early shot generation all tournament)
